aboutPreview.title: A Journey of Reconstruction & Development
aboutPreview.p1: Since the rebuilding of the Golden Dome in 2009, the Al-Askari Holy Shrine has continued relentless reconstruction and development efforts. Dozens of major projects have been completed, transforming the Shrine into a world-class architectural landmark befitting its sacred status.
aboutPreview.p2: Our ongoing and planned projects include expanding the sacred courtyard, developing visitor reception areas, and constructing modern service facilities to accommodate the growing numbers of pilgrims from around the world.

about.historyP1: The Shrine of Imams Ali al-Hadi and Hasan al-Askari (Peace Be Upon Them) is located in the holy city of Samarra, and is one of the holiest Islamic shrines in the world. The shrine houses the remains of the 10th Imam Ali al-Hadi and the 11th Imam Hasan al-Askari.
about.historyP2: The shrine was built in 944 CE, and is distinguished by its famous golden dome, which was covered with 72,000 gold pieces in 1905, making it a prominent symbol of the holy city of Samarra and a global Islamic landmark.
about.historyP3: The shrine also houses the tomb of Lady Hakima Khatun (sister of Imam Ali al-Hadi) and Lady Narjis Khatun (mother of Imam Muhammad al-Mahdi).
about.reconstructionP1: The holy shrine was subjected to two terrorist bombings in 2006 and 2007, which destroyed the golden dome and two historic minarets. This heinous attack shook the feelings of Muslims around the world.
about.reconstructionP2: Reconstruction efforts began in 2009 with support from the Iraqi government, UNESCO, the United Nations Development Programme, under the guidance of Grand Ayatollah Sayyid Ali al-Sistani.
about.reconstructionP3: The golden dome and minarets were rebuilt in their full glory, and the Holy Shrine continues ongoing maintenance and development efforts to serve visitors.

about.donationsText: Your generous donations fund restoration, reconstruction and expansion projects, contribute to infrastructure modernization, develop courtyards and service facilities, and preserve the Islamic architectural heritage for future generations.

footer.about: Shrine of Imams Ali al-Hadi and Hasan al-Askari (Peace Be Upon Them) in the holy city of Samarra. We strive to reconstruct the Holy Shrine and develop its facilities to serve pilgrims.
